rathakārakule.meaning: in a chariot-maker's clan; in a family of carriage builders
breakdown: In a clan, family (locative of kula).

rathassāṇīva.meaning: like the axle-pin of a chariot
rathassāṇīva.gram: phrase in sandhi: genitive + feminine noun + comparison particle
breakdown: Like, as (iva shortened); the vowels fuse at the boundaries.
rathassāṇīva.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (raho “secret”); the phrase itself means “like the linchpin of a chariot”.

rathavaramāruyha.gram: phrase in sandhi: noun + adjective (accusative) + gerund
breakdown: The niggahīta joins the following vowel as m.

rattindivamatandito.meaning: diligent night and day; tireless by night and day
breakdown: The niggahīta joins the following vowel as m.

rattūparato.meaning: abstaining (from food) at night; having ceased by night
breakdown: Abstained from, ceased (past participle of uparamati).

ratā.gram: past participle of ramati (√ram + ta), nominative plural (or feminine singular)

re.meaning: hey!; hello; go on! (exclamation, often contemptuous)

rittaṁ.gram: past participle of riñcati (√ric + ta), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to empty, to leave behind”.

rocaye.meaning: would find pleasure in; should approve of
breakdown: Optative ending of the causative stem.

roceti.meaning: is pleased with; agrees to, approves
roceti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of √ruc

rodantiyā.meaning: of/for the weeping (woman); while she was crying
rodantiyā.gram: present participle of rodati, feminine oblique singular

rogāmanussa-dubbhikkhaṁ.meaning: disease, non-human beings (evil spirits) and famine (the three dangers dispelled at Vesāli)
breakdown: Non-human being, spirit (roga + amanussa coalesce to rogāmanussa).

rohinītherīgāthā.meaning: the Verses of the Elder Nun Rohinī (Rohinītherīgāthā)

rukkhamūle.meaning: at the root of a tree; at the foot of a tree

rāgadosaparetehi.meaning: by those overcome by craving and aversion
rāgadosaparetehi.goenka: Rāga (craving) and dosa (aversion) are the two root reactions out of which all mental defilement grows. One overcome by them keeps generating saṅkhāra and suffering; the whole work of vipassana is to observe the sensations on which craving and aversion feed with equanimity, so that these two are gradually eradicated rather than multiplied.

breakdown: Destruction, exhaustion; sandhi doubling of k at the boundary.
rāgakkhayo.goenka: Rāgakkhayo, the complete destruction of craving — together with the destruction of aversion (dosakkhayo) and delusion (mohakkhayo) — is a canonical definition of nibbāna itself. It is not a suppression but a full eradication, accomplished by the meditator who observes every arising and passing sensation without reacting.

rāgapariyuṭṭhitaṁ.meaning: pervaded by craving; possessed, obsessed by passion
breakdown: Linking glide at the vowel boundary.
Risen up, taken hold (past participle of uṭṭhahati): risen up all around, pervading.

rāgavinayapariyosānā.meaning: terminating in the driving away of craving; culminating in the removal of greed
rāgavinayapariyosānā.goenka: In the Brahmayāna passage each factor of the Noble Eightfold Path, when developed and cultivated, “terminates in driving away greed, in driving away ill will, in driving away delusion”. The path is thus measured by its result: every step is for the actual removal of rāga, dosa and moha.

rājaporise.meaning: in the king's service; among government men
breakdown: Man, servant (from purisa): in the service of the king.

rūpanibbattipassanākārakathā, visuddhima.meaning: the “Discussion of the Mode of Seeing the Arising of Materiality” (a section of the Visuddhimagga)
rūpanibbattipassanākārakathā, visuddhima.gram: proper noun (commentarial section title), feminine singular
breakdown: The Path of Purification, Buddhaghosa's commentary.

rūpasañcetanā.meaning: volition regarding visible forms; intention directed towards forms

rūpattāya.meaning: for formhood; for the state of (having) form, shaping
rūpattāya.gram: neuter abstract noun rūpatta, dative/genitive singular

rūpā.gram: neuter noun rūpa, nominative plural (or ablative singular)
rūpā.goenka: Rūpa is matter, the material form — the first of the five aggregates and one half of mind-and-matter (nāma-rūpa). In vipassana the entire field of rūpa is explored within the framework of one's own body, where its arising and passing is experienced directly as sensations.

sa.meaning: true, real (prefixed form of sant, as in saddhamma “the true Dhamma”)
sa.gram: prefix (reduced form of sant, present participle of atthi “to be”)
breakdown: Being, existing, true (present participle of atthi); by sandhi sant > sat > sad before dhamma.
Sandhi doubling at the compound boundary (sad + dhamma > saddhamma).
sa.goenka: Klaus notes the derivation of saddhamma: sa + d + dhamma, “with true dhamma”. The element sa- is literally sant, the present participle of atthi “to be, existing” — what truly exists, the real. By the sandhi rules sant develops through sat and sad to give saddhamma, the true teaching.

sabalampi.meaning: even spotted; though blotched, inconsistent (of sīla)
sabalampi.gram: adjective sabala + emphatic particle pi, accusative singular
breakdown: Even, also (api); the niggahīta becomes m before p.

sabbabhūtesu.meaning: towards all beings; among all creatures

sabbakilesabyādhiparipīḷitaṁ.meaning: oppressed by the disease of all the mental impurities

sabbaphāliphullā.meaning: all in full blossom; everywhere burst into flower
sabbaphāliphullā.gram: adjective, compound, nominative plural (or feminine singular)

sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī.meaning: compassionate for the welfare of all breathing beings
breakdown: Compassionate towards (hita + anukampī coalesce to hitānukampī).

sabbasantharisanthataṁ.meaning: spread all over with coverings; fully carpeted and prepared

sabbasaṅkhārasamatho.meaning: the calming of all mental conditioning; the stilling of all saṅkhāra
sabbasaṅkhārasamatho.goenka: Sabbasaṅkhārasamatho, the calming of all saṅkhāra, appears in the canonical praise of nibbāna: “this is peaceful, this is sublime — the stilling of all conditioning, the relinquishment of all attachment, the destruction of craving, dispassion, cessation, nibbāna.” Saṅkhāra here is the mental preconditioning, the conditioned reactions accumulating mostly unnoticed below the threshold of consciousness; their complete stilling, experienced by the meditator who has ceased generating new reactions, is liberation itself.

sabbasetena.meaning: all white; with an entirely white (chariot)

sabbathā.meaning: in all ways; in every manner

sabbato.gram: indeclinable adverb (ablative of sabba used adverbially)

sabbattatāya.meaning: identifying all beings with oneself; with compassion towards all equally, everywhere
sabbattatāya.gram: indeclinable adverb / feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular

sabbatthamaparājitā.gram: adjective phrase in sandhi, nominative plural
breakdown: Linking consonant at the vowel boundary.

sabbatthasotthiṁ.meaning: safety everywhere; well-being in every place

sabbo.meaning: all; the whole (he in his entirety, with so: that whole person)

sabbo so.meaning: he in his entirety; that whole person
sabbo so.gram: phrase: pronoun sabba + demonstrative so, nominative masculine singular
